title: Upon Pearch. Epig.
content:
Thou writes in prose how sweet all virgins be;
    But there's not one, doth praise the smell of thee.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Pearch. Epig.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
